directions.name: Non-square tiles
directions.summary: Ti=8,Tj=4,Tk=1 non-square tiles. Superseded by rank2/sA-cache.

directions.name: sA-cache single-cell + sB scratchpad
directions.summary: Cache A[ii,bk] in addr 1 (cost 1) before inner jj loop. sB at 3-6, sC at 7-38. Analytically proven optimal for this family.

directions.name: A-tile reuse across bj (loop reorder bi>bk>bj)
directions.summary: Expensive bulk-C reload kills the savings from fewer A copies.

directions.name: Strassen 2x2 within tiles
directions.summary: M-values at addr 50+ (cost 8) too expensive to read repeatedly.

directions.name: Strassen with M-values at addr 1-7
directions.summary: Place all 7 Strassen M-values at addr 1-7 (cost 1-3). Each M value used 2-3 times in outputs.
directions.prompt:
Strassen's algorithm multiplies 2x2 matrices with 7 products instead of 8.

Formula (A=[[a,b],[c,d]], B=[[e,f],[g,h]]):
  M1=(a+d)*(e+h)  → C[0,0], C[1,1]
  M2=(c+d)*e      → C[1,0], C[1,1]
  M3=a*(f-h)      → C[0,1], C[1,1]
  M4=d*(g-e)      → C[0,0], C[1,0]
  M5=(a+b)*h      → C[0,0], C[0,1]
  M6=(c-a)*(e+f)  → C[1,1]
  M7=(b-d)*(g+h)  → C[0,0]
  C[0,0]=M1+M4-M5+M7, C[0,1]=M3+M5, C[1,0]=M2+M4, C[1,1]=M1-M2+M3+M6

Key: place M1..M7 at addr 1-7 (cost 1-3). Place intermediate sums at addr 8-14.
Tile 16x16 into 2x2 blocks (nb=8). For each 2x2 output block, accumulate
Strassen products across all 8 bk reduction blocks. Previous Strassen attempt
failed because M-values were at addr 50+ (cost 8) — low addresses are the fix.

directions.name: Stream single A column (Ti=16, Tj=1, Tk=1)
directions.summary: Cache A[:,k] in addr 2-17, load B[k,j] to addr 1, compute full output column. Each A value reused 16 times at cost 2-5.
directions.prompt:
For each k (0..15):
  1. Copy A[:,k] (16 values) into sA_col at addr 2-17 (cost 2-5).
     This column is reused for all 16 j values.
  2. For each j (0..15):
     a. Copy B[k,j] to addr 1 (cost 1) — 1 bulk read per j.
     b. For each i (0..15): mul tmp, sA_col[i], B_cache; add/init C_scratch[i]
  3. After all k: write C_scratch accumulated values to bulk C.

Each A[i,k] read 16 times from sA at cost 2-5 (much cheaper than bulk cost 5-17).
Each B[k,j] read 16 times from addr 1 at cost 1.
C accumulator at addr 18-33 (cost 5-6).

directions.name: Three-level cascade: 16x16 -> 4x4 -> 2x2
directions.summary: Innermost 2x2 scratchpad at addr 1-4 (cost 1-2), loaded from 4x4 mid-scratchpad, loaded from bulk.
directions.prompt:
Three tiling levels:
  Level 3 (innermost): 2x2 computation. sA at addr 2-5, sB at 6-9, sC at 10-13, tmp at 1.
  Level 2 (mid): 4x4 tile scratchpad at addr 14-45 (cost 4-7).
  Level 1 (outer): bulk at addr 46+.

Each inner 2x2 read happens at cost 1-4. The extra copy overhead at the mid
level should be outweighed by cheaper inner arithmetic reads. Implement and
measure the breakdown at each level.

directions.name: Algebraic pre-summation to reduce mul reads
directions.summary: Pre-compute pairwise sums/differences (free additions) before multiplications to reduce number of distinct source reads.
directions.prompt:
Additions are FREE. Use this to reduce the number of distinct addresses read by muls.

For each 2x2 sub-block, pre-compute:
  (a+d), (c+d), (a+b), (c-a), (b-d), (f-h), (g-e), (e+h), (e+f), (g+h)
as additions (free, 1 write each). Then do 7 Strassen muls reading only these
pre-computed values from low-cost addresses.

The key: pre-computed sums live at addr 1-10 (cost 1-4) and are each read exactly
once. Strassen M-values at addr 11-17 (cost 4), each read 2-3 times.
Compare total cost vs direct Strassen without pre-sums.
